knowledge_base_meta.project: GharDisha AI — AI Climate-Displacement Case Interpreter for PMAY-G Navigation
knowledge_base_meta.purpose: Curated source-grounded snippets for RAG retrieval. All wording is deliberately conservative ('may', 'depends on official verification') to support responsible-AI requirements and avoid guarantee language.
knowledge_base_meta.verification_note: Facts cross-checked against PIB releases and MoRD sources as of June 2026. Re-confirm time-sensitive details on official PMAY-G, MoRD, and PIB sources before production use. Treat all output as source-grounded guidance for official verification, never as a verdict.

snippets.text: PMAY-G provides unit assistance of Rs. 1.20 lakh in plain areas and Rs. 1.30 lakh in North-Eastern states, hilly states, J&K/Ladakh, and difficult areas. The amount supports construction of a pucca house and is not a guaranteed grant until official verification is complete.
snippets.source_name: Press Information Bureau, Ministry of Rural Development

snippets.text: The unit cost is shared between Centre and State at 60:40 for plain areas, 90:10 for North-Eastern and Himalayan states, and is 100% centrally funded for Union Territories without a legislature.

In addition to unit assistance, a PMAY-G beneficiary may receive approximately 90 to 95 person-days of unskilled wage labour through MGNREGS for house construction, and toilet support of Rs. 12,000 through Swachh Bharat Mission-Gramin or other convergence sources. These are facilitated through the Gram Panchayat and depend on official linkage of the housing case.

To be considered under PMAY-G, a household should generally be houseless or living in a kutcha or dilapidated house with zero, one, or two rooms, should not own a pucca house anywhere in the country, and should be a resident of the rural area where assistance is sought. Meeting these conditions does not by itself confirm eligibility; final selection depends on official data, Gram Sabha verification, and approval.

PMAY-G beneficiary identification is based on rural housing-deprivation parameters and exclusion criteria, drawing on SECC 2011 data and the Awaas+ list, followed by Gram Sabha verification and an appellate process. A household not already on the existing list may need to be captured through the official survey/updation process before it can be considered.
snippets.source_name: Press Information Bureau, Ministry of Rural Development ('Transparency and Accountability under PMAY-G')

snippets.text: Awaas+ 2024 is the updation/survey exercise used to identify additional potentially eligible rural households for the 2024-29 phase of PMAY-G, through self-survey and Panchayat-assisted survey using the official Awaas+ 2024 mobile application. Being surveyed does not guarantee inclusion; records move through verification before any approval. A newly displaced family may need to check whether it is captured in Awaas+/SECC or requires a fresh survey and local verification.
snippets.source_name: MoRD SOP for Self-Survey using Awaas+ 2024 Application

snippets.text: Under the 2024 modified exclusion criteria, several earlier disqualifiers were removed — owning a mechanised two-wheeler, a mechanised fishing boat, a landline phone, or a refrigerator no longer automatically excludes a household. Certain income, land, and asset conditions can still affect eligibility or exclusion and are checked through the official process.
snippets.source_name: Press Information Bureau, Ministry of Rural Development ('Modified/automatic exclusion criteria, PMAY-G')

snippets.text: The Union Cabinet approved an expansion of PMAY-G covering FY 2024-25 to FY 2028-29 for the construction of 2 crore additional rural houses, with a total outlay of about Rs. 3.06 lakh crore. The scheme is active and expanding.
snippets.source_name: Press Information Bureau, Ministry of Rural Development

snippets.text: Assistance is released in installments through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to the beneficiary's Aadhaar-linked bank or post-office account. Aadhaar linkage and bank account readiness are practical prerequisites a family can prepare in advance.

GharDisha AI does not determine final PMAY-G eligibility or approval. Final decisions remain with the Gram Sabha, the Block Development Officer (BDO), and official government verification. GharDisha AI only helps a family understand the system and prepare for that human process.

A household temporarily staying with relatives, neighbours, a school shelter, or a relief shelter after disaster damage is not automatically eligible and not automatically rejected under PMAY-G. Officials must verify the household's own housing status, whether it owns any pucca house anywhere, whether the current shelter is temporary, whether the family appears in SECC/Awaas+ or needs survey/updation, and whether damage or displacement proof is available. Wrong or false information can be rejected during Gram Sabha, BDO, and official verification.
snippets.source_name: PMAY-G beneficiary verification process (cross-referenced, MoRD / PIB)

ai_output_safety_rules: Always use 'you may qualify' or 'may be eligible' — never 'you qualify' or 'you are eligible'.
State the basis of a decision (the rule and its source), never the verdict.
Never present income/land/asset thresholds as a pass/fail calculator; frame them as conditions the official process verifies.
Never claim displacement creates automatic priority or automatic inclusion unless an official source is cited.
Every output ends with the human-decision boundary (Gram Sabha / BDO / official verification).
Do not display a specific date or helpline number that has not been verified against an official source.
